arts.ttl: Real Madrid vs Arsenal: how to watch the UCL second leg on TV, stream it online in the U.S.
arts.sum: Champions League holders Real Madrid are tasked with overturning a three-goal deficit against Arsenal, after Declan Rice’s free-kick heroics led the Gunners to a comprehensive win in last week’s quarter-final first leg. In the return leg of the 2024/25 Champions League quarter-finals, Madrid host Arsenal at the Estadio Bernabéu on Wednesday, April 16, 2025 .

arts.ttl: EXCLUSIVE | Emmanuel Petit: “Vinicius Jr. last season was fighting for the Ballon d’Or. Where is he?”
arts.sum: Emmanuel Petit sat down with Get French Football News to discuss his former side, Arsenal, ahead of their Champions League quarter-final against Real Madrid. Arsenal travel to the Santiago Bernabéu…

arts.ttl: Real Madrid team news: 'Decision made' on Kylian Mbappe's suspension following red card against Alaves
arts.sum: Real Madrid's Kylian Mbappe will reportedly serve a one-match ban following his red card against Alaves, making him available for the Copa del Rey final at the end of the month.

arts.ttl: Why Kylian Mbappe is available to face Arsenal despite Real Madrid red card
arts.sum: Why Kylian Mbappe is free to face Arsenal despite receiving a suspension for a red card against Alaves.

arts.ttl: Every word Mikel Arteta said on Real Madrid comeback, fear, Thomas Partey, injury and Declan Rice
arts.sum: That emotional aspect is very significant because I have a lot of reassurance for what the team has done all season, in a positive context and in a very difficult context, which I think is probably the pathway and why those things happen. So to prepare to a semi-final like this and to be convinced that we can handle any situation and we're going to enjoy actually playing any kind of game that we have to be thrown tomorrow.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta reveals how fearless Arsenal will approach Real Madrid showdown
arts.sum: Gunners head to the Bernabeu with a commanding 3-0 advantage as they look to reach the Champions League semi-finals

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta's Arsenal legacy "up there" with Arsene Wenger if Gunners win Champions League
arts.sum: Arsenal winning the Champions League would "elevate" Mikel Arteta to Arsene Wenger and George Graham levels of fame, Gunners legend Nigel Winterburn tells Sports Mole.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta provides triple Arsenal injury update before Real Madrid
arts.sum: Arsenal head coach Mikel Arteta has provided an update on the fitness situation in his squad. This of course comes ahead of the Gunners’ highly-anticipated upcoming clash with Real Madrid.…

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta urges Arsenal to be 'bold and brave' against Real Madrid - as he insists the Gunners will play to WIN in Spain despite 3-0 aggregate lead
arts.sum: ISAAN KHAN IN MADRID: Mikel Arteta has roared on his Arsenal side to be 'bold and brave' when they face-off against Real Madrid at the intimidating Bernabeu tonight.

arts.ttl: ‘Impossible’ for Arsenal to block out Real Madrid narrative, admits Arteta
arts.sum: Talk of ‘remontada’ is rife despite 3-0 first-leg deficit Arteta wants Arsenal to try to ‘dominate’ in Europe Real Madrid’s history has loomed large with its epic comebacks but now it is time for Arsenal to write their own, Mikel Arteta says. For all the noise and the narrative, for all that many have crumbled here against the European champions, the Arsenal manager insisted there is no fear and that his team are ready to step into the Bernabéu. Arteta reminded everyone of a simple fact lost at times over the past seven days: they do lead this tie, 3‑0. “If I had to pick one of the scenarios that I want the most, it would be this one,” he said.
